mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of periods following inflation spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a frequency exceeding 57% over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad economic growth that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across rolling 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth fails to predict negative returns with a frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons.
